req.title: System-Admin views department
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a department through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-008 requires a system-admin to view a department via UI, with mandatory field validation, audit record creation, and appropriate HTTP responses. Test coverage is designed using EP (valid admin viewing a real department), NEG (unauthenticated and forbidden-role access), BVA (department name at maximum allowed length boundary), STC (state transition: department transitions from created to viewed with audit state written), and DT (decision table covering auth/role combinations). Each test is self-contained using fixtures and helper placeholders to keep executables concise and deterministic.

tests.name: test_admin_views_existing_department_returns_200_and_audit
preconditions: A department record exists in the system
actions: Locate and click the existing department row
Assert department detail form is visible
expected: Department detail form rendered with correct department data
Audit record written with actor=system-admin and action=VIEW
trace: R-WEB-008: Expected outcomes - department is viewed
R-WEB-008: Expected outcomes - audit record written
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_views_existing_department_returns_200_and_audit(
    admin_page,
    existing_department,
    audit_client
):
    page = admin_page
    dept_id = existing_department["id"]
    dept_name = existing_department["name"]

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/departments" in r.url and r.status == 200
    ) as resp_info:
        page.goto("/departments")

    assert resp_info.value.status == 200

    row = page.locator(f"[data-dept-id='{dept_id}']")
    expect(row).to_be_visible()
    row.click()

    detail_form = page.locator("[data-testid='department-detail-form']")
    expect(detail_form).to_be_visible()
    expect(detail_form.locator("[data-field='name']")).to_have_text(dept_name)

    audit_records = audit_client.get_records(
        entity_id=dept_id, action="VIEW"
    )
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1
    assert audit_records[-1]["actor_role"] == "admin"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_viewing_department_returns_401
preconditions: No active session / user is not authenticated
actions: Clear any existing session cookies via <clear_session_fixture>
Send GET request to /departments without auth token
Assert no department data is returned
expected: Response body contains error indicating unauthenticated
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_viewing_department_returns_401(
    base_url,
    clear_session
):
    clear_session()

    response = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/departments",
        allow_redirects=False
    )

    assert response.status_code in (401, 302), (
        f"Expected 401 or auth redirect, got {response.status_code}"
    )

    if response.status_code == 401:
        body = response.json()
        assert "error" in body or "message" in body
        assert "id" not in body
        assert "departments" not in body

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_viewing_department_returns_403
actions: Send GET request to /departments with non-admin token
Assert no department data is returned
expected: No department entity id in response
trace: R-WEB-008: Error pathways - 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_forbidden_role_viewing_department_returns_403(
    base_url,
    non_admin_token
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {non_admin_token}"}

    response = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/departments",
        headers=headers,
        allow_redirects=False
    )

    assert response.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403 for forbidden role, got {response.status_code}"
    )

    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body
    dept_data = body.get("data") or body.get("departments")
    assert not dept_data, "No department data should be returned for forbidden role"

tests.name: test_admin_views_department_with_max_length_name
preconditions: A department exists with a name exactly at the maximum allowed length (255 chars)
actions: Ensure a department with a 255-character name exists via <seed_department_fixture>
Locate the department row by id
Assert department name displays fully without truncation error
expected: Department detail form rendered with full 255-character name
No validation error or overflow error displayed
trace: R-WEB-008: Mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-008: Expected outcomes - department is viewed
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MAX_NAME_LEN = 255

def test_admin_views_department_with_max_length_name(
    admin_page,
    seed_department,
    audit_client
):
    max_name = "D" * MAX_NAME_LEN
    dept = seed_department(name=max_name)
    dept_id = dept["id"]

    page = admin_page
    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/departments" in r.url and r.status == 200
    ):
        page.goto("/departments")

    row = page.locator(f"[data-dept-id='{dept_id}']")
    expect(row).to_be_visible()
    row.click()

    detail_form = page.locator("[data-testid='department-detail-form']")
    expect(detail_form).to_be_visible()

    name_field = detail_form.locator("[data-field='name']")
    displayed_name = name_field.inner_text()
    assert len(displayed_name) == MAX_NAME_LEN
    assert displayed_name == max_name

    error_banner = page.locator("[data-testid='validation-error']").count()
    assert error_banner == 0

    records = audit_client.get_records(entity_id=dept_id, action="VIEW")
    assert len(records) >= 1

tests.name: test_admin_views_department_state_transition_creates_audit
preconditions: No prior VIEW audit record exists for this department by this admin in this session
actions: Record audit count before viewing via <audit_client>
Navigate to /departments and open department detail
Assert audit count increased by exactly 1 with action=VIEW
expected: Audit record count increments by 1 after viewing
New audit record has action=VIEW, entity_type=department, actor_role=admin
State transition: department moves from UNVIEWED to VIEWED in session audit trail
trace: R-WEB-008: Expected outcomes - audit record written
R-WEB-008: Triggers - system-admin views a department
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_views_department_state_transition_creates_audit(
    admin_page,
    existing_department,
    audit_client
):
    dept_id = existing_department["id"]

    pre_count = len(
        audit_client.get_records(entity_id=dept_id, action="VIEW")
    )

    page = admin_page
    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/departments" in r.url and r.status == 200
    ) as resp_info:
        page.goto("/departments")

    assert resp_info.value.status == 200

    row = page.locator(f"[data-dept-id='{dept_id}']")
    expect(row).to_be_visible()
    row.click()

    expect(
        page.locator("[data-testid='department-detail-form']")
    ).to_be_visible()

    post_records = audit_client.get_records(entity_id=dept_id, action="VIEW")
    post_count = len(post_records)

    assert post_count == pre_count + 1
    latest = post_records[-1]
    assert latest["action"] == "VIEW"
    assert latest["entity_type"] == "department"
    assert latest["actor_role"] == "admin"
